lede: A look at February 14 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Michael Bloomberg and Bacary Sagna, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Arab Spring
events.desc: As a part of Arab Spring, the Bahraini uprising begins with a 'Day of Rage'.

events.title: 2008 Northern Illinois University shooting
events.desc: Northern Illinois University shooting: A gunman opens fire in a lecture hall of Northern Illinois University in DeKalb County, Illinois, resulting in six fatalities (including the gunman) and 21 injuries.

events.title: YouTube
events.desc: YouTube is launched by a group of college students, eventually becoming the largest video sharing website in the world and a main source for viral videos.

events.title: Moscow
events.desc: In a suburb of Moscow, Russia, the roof of the Transvaal water park collapses, killing more than 28 people, and wounding 193 others.

events.title: Indian Airlines Flight 605
events.desc: Ninety-two people are killed when Indian Airlines Flight 605 crashes in Bangalore, India.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: German battleship Bismarck is launched.

events.title: Great Ormond Street Hospital
events.desc: Great Ormond St Hospital for Sick Children, the first hospital in England to provide in-patient beds specifically for children, is founded in London.

events.title: Karađorđe
events.desc: Karađorđe leads the First Serbian Uprising against the Ottoman Empire.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: The Battle of Kettle Creek is fought in Georgia.

events.title: Death of James Cook
events.desc: James Cook is killed by Native Hawaiians near Kealakekua on the Island of Hawaii.

births.name: Michael Bloomberg
births.line: American businessman and politician, 108th Mayor of New York City

births.name: Mark Rutte
births.line: Dutch businessman and politician, Prime Minister of the Netherlands

births.name: Alexander Kluge
births.line: German author, film director and public intellectual

deaths.text: Adolph Dubs, American lieutenant and diplomat, United States Ambassador to Afghanistan
David Hilbert, Russian-German mathematician, physicist, and philosopher
Eugène Charles Catalan, Belgian-French mathematician and academic
James Cook, English captain, cartographer, and explorer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Cyril and Methodius, patron saints of Europe (Roman Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Valentine (see also Valentine's Day)
